title: PBMC Isolation & Cryopreservation from Whole Blood
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5fomj","text":"The purpose of this procedure is to isolate and cryopreserve peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC's) from 30 mL of freshly drawn whole blood.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Place RPMI media and Ficoll in water bath. Bring to Room temperature ( 19 °C - 25 °C ).
Spin tubes at 1850 rpm, °C, 15 minutes (breaks off; 5 acc 0 brake) for plasma removal. Total time for spin including acceleration and deceleration – 00:30:00 .
Pipette 15 mL, plasma from the spun blood. Leave ~ 1 mL - 2 mL, plasma above the buffy coat/RBC layers.
Store sample ID labeled Plasma aliquot in the -80˚C freezer.
Prepare two sterile 50 mL conical tubes and label with sample ID.
Use pipette gun to distribute 15 mL, Ficoll into each tube.

Use a maximum of 10-12 Ficoll tubes at one single time. If more samples – repeat step 5-9 with remainder while the first batch waits for step 10 (Fourth spin). Try to limit the time the cells are in contact with Ficoll.
Aspirate remaining plasma with Pasteur pipette vacuum from all blood tubes until 1 mL - 2 mL of plasma remain above RBC layers.
Dilute remaining blood in all tubes with RPMI media to 50 mL.
Gently pipet diluted blood to homogenize. Important to pipet slowly.
Use pipette gun to slowly dispense 35 mL, diluted blood onto each Ficoll tube, without breaking the interface or mixing. ( 35 mL, diluted blood to 15 mL, Ficoll )
Use small amount of RPMI media to rinse empty blood tubes and overlay over Ficoll.
Secure all caps to avoid leaks.
Spin tubes at 1850 rpm, °C, 25 minutes (breaks off). Total time for spin including acceleration and deceleration – 00:40:00 .
Prepare and label new sterile conical tube for PBMC’s; 2 isolated layers per conical tube (i.e. 2 ficoll tubes / 2 layers = 1 50ml conical needed) .
Retrieve Ficoll tubes from centrifuge. Tubes will have four distinct layers.
Aspirate media/plasma (top layer) from all tubes until 10 mL remain over PBMC buffy coat layer.
Pipette PBMC buffy coat layers into sterile 50 mL conical tubes (combine 2 isolated layers per tube).
Rinse conical tube containing PBMC’s with RPMI media by adding RPMI to 50 mL.
Spin tubes at 1850 rpm, °C, 10 minutes (breaks low).
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend cell pellets in 10 mL, RPMI media .
Wash cells by diluting to 50 mL with RPMI media.
Spin tubes at 1850 rpm, °C, 10 minutes (breaks ON; acceleration at 5).
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend cell pellets in 10 mL, RPMI media .
Combine cell pellets into one conical tube and dilute cell suspension to 30 mL.
Cap and invert tube to ensure cell suspension is homogenous.
Use p100 micropipette and aliquot 20 μL, cell suspension for counting.
Do a 1:2 dilution with 0.1 %, trypan blue and count in hemocytometer (i.e. 20 μL, cell suspension with 20 μL, 0.1% trypan blue ).
Spin tubes at 800 rpm, °C, 10 minutes (breaks OFF).
Print labels with sample ID, cell density, date, and initial. Prepare and label cryovials and Mr. Frosty container.
Freeze at a density of ~10 million cells per vial with a maximum of 3 vials per sample for 30 ml sample.
Aspirate supernatant.

Resuspend cell pellet in appropriate amount of fridge cold Freezing media (FBS with 10 %, DMSO ) at 1 mL per vial (i.e. 3 vials = resuspend in 3 mL, FBS ) . Important to work quickly and in batches for this step. DMSO is toxic for the cells.
Repeat step 5-9 as needed depending on number of samples being processed simultaneously.
Count the viable cells in 4 corner squares and middle square of hemocytometer.
Count the number of dead (blue) cells in the same squares.
Calculate cell density, total cells yielded and viability (no. of dead cells divided by total number of cells).
Calculate number of cryovials needed based on specified freezing density.
** pipette cell suspension into cryovials quickly and efficiently to prevent loss of viability.
Aim for 8-10 million cells per vial. - 15 million or less cells – 1 vial, - 16-23 million cells – 2 vials, - 24 million and above – 3 vials.
Place cryovials in frosty container and freeze in -80˚C freezer Overnight ( 24:00:00 ).
Transfer cells to nitrogen tank for long term storage (work quickly to avoid temperature changes).
creator: Ngan Kim Tran
